mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is trending down. The signal fails if rate cuts are already fully priced or if equity markets are reacting to recession risk faster than discount-rate relief.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive on average during FEDFUNDS downtrends, or if the sign of the effect flips in a rolling 3-year window with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a short-run threshold marks worsening labor market stress and increasing recession probability, which should pressure equities lower over the next month. The signal is strongest when the unemployment rate has just crossed into a new higher plateau rather than when it is already elevated and stable.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average triggered return is indistinguishable from zero at p <= 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both strengthen together, it indicates real final demand and payroll income are accelerating at the same time, which should support equity earnings expectations and lift SPX over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if sales improve without labor income growth or if labor gains are offset by margin compression.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS growth > 0 and PAYEMS growth > 0 does not outperform either proxy alone on 60d SPX returns, or if interaction IC stays below 0.03 for 3 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or improving GDPC1 signals broad real output recovery rather than a price-only rebound, which should produce positive equity returns over the next month. The effect fails if industrial output rises because of inventory restocking while final demand remains weak.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive INDPRO growth and non-declining GDPC1 do not show higher 20d SPX returns than unconditional samples, or if the joint signal is negative in more than 40% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero bound, real policy rates fall further and liquidity conditions stay easy, which should support SPX gains over the next 5 trading days. The effect fails if inflation surprises are interpreted as growth-negative shock rather than liquidity-positive impulse.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered windows do not show positive 5d SPX returns on average, or if inflation acceleration at the zero bound fails to outperform non-triggered periods by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp HOUST decline below trend signals weakening housing demand and broader cyclical deceleration, which should weigh on equities over the following 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if lower housing starts are driven by supply constraints while other demand indicators remain strong.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-trend episodes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the mean return is not negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deterioration in PAYEMS and rising UNRATE identifies genuine labor market slack, which should predict negative SPX returns over a 60-day horizon. The signal fails when unemployment rises only because labor force participation changes without payroll losses.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not lead to below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not negative in rolling 3-year estimations.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDP growth surprise in GDPC1 combined with stable consumer prices in CPIAUCSL implies real activity is improving without forcing an inflation shock, which should lift SPX over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if GDP growth is driven by inventory noise or if inflation accelerates enough to offset real growth.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth with stable CPIAUCSL does not improve 60d SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the sign turns negative in periods of CPI acceleration.
